q: There are several alternative and renewable sources of energy which are not only environment friendly but can also be available in _.
Sun is one of the most powerful _ sources of energy.
Solar energy is the _ light and heat from the sun that has been harnessed by humans since ancient times.
Earth's atmosphere and clouds absorb or scatter as much as _ % of the incoming sunlight.
Above the earth's atmosphere, solar radiation has an intensity of approximately _ $\text{W m}^{-2}$.
The value of solar radiation intensity above earth's atmosphere is known as the solar _.
Another commonly used term for irradiance of sunlight is _.
Insolation is the solar irradiance received on a _ surface such as ground.
At the earth's surface solar radiation has an intensity of approximately _ $\text{W m}^{-2}$ on a clear day at solar noon in the summer.
Conversion of light energy into electrical energy is based on a phenomenon called _ effect.
A solar cell is basically a _ junction diode.
Individual solar cells can be combined to form modules commonly known as solar _.
Achieving the perfect _ sun rays is the optimal orientation so that solar panels receive direct light throughout the day.
Power conversion efficiency of the solar panel is the ratio of the electrical power delivered by the panel to the solar power _ by it.
Solar heating can also play an important role in reducing the impact of global _.
Flat plate collectors have _ pipes attached to an absorber plate contained in an insulated box.
Concentrated solar power (CSP) systems generate solar power by using mirrors or _ to concentrate a large area of sunlight onto a specific point of receiver.
A wind turbine converts wind energy into _, which can then be used to power electrical equipment, stored in batteries or transmitted over power lines.
Wind energy is the energy generated or produced by harnessing the power of the ___.
Like solar power, harnessing the wind is highly dependent on weather and ___.
Windmills convert wind energy directly into ___ energy for tasks such as milling grain.
A wind turbine converts wind energy into ___.
Wind farms consist of many individual wind turbines, which are connected to the electric power ___ network.
Wind energy is the ___ energy of air in motion.
In the equation for wind energy, $E = \frac{1}{2}mv^2$, the variable $\rho$ represents the ___ of air.
The output of a wind turbine is proportional to the dimensions of the rotor and to the ___ of the wind speed.
Theoretically, when wind speed doubles, wind power potential increases by a factor of ___.
Wind turbines, like windmills, are mounted on a ___ to capture the most energy.
At 100 feet (30 meters) or more above ground, turbines can take advantage of the faster and less ___ wind.
Usually, two or three blades are mounted on a shaft to form a ___.
Hydropower, or hydroelectric power, is one of the oldest and largest sources of ___ energy.
The source of power in a hydroelectric plant is the ___ of water.
There are three types of hydropower facilities: impoundment, diversion, and ___ storage.
The most common type of hydroelectric power plant is an ___ facility.
Water is stored at a higher elevation and gravity pulls the water downward through large pipes or tunnels called ___.
In an impoundment system, the difference in the two water elevations is known as the ___.
Transformers are used to convert the alternating voltage of the generators to a ___ voltage suitable for long-distance transmission.
A penstock is a closed ___ that channels the flow of water to turbines.
A diversion, sometimes called a ___-of-river facility, channels a portion of a river through a canal or a penstock.
When the demand for electricity is low, a PSH facility stores energy by pumping water from the lower reservoir to an ___ reservoir.
In the hydroelectric power equation $P = \eta \rho \dot{V} g h$, the variable $\eta$ represents the ___ of the turbine.
The variable $\dot{V}$ in the power equation represents the ___ flow rate.
A water turbine converts the kinetic energy of falling water into ___ energy.
Due to the gravitational pull of mainly the ___ on the spinning earth, the level of water in the sea rises and falls.
The tidal energy is produced by the difference in sea ___.
Tidal energy is harnessed by constructing a dam, called a tidal ___, across a narrow opening to the sea.
The kinetic energy of huge waves near the seashore is generated by strong ___ blowing across the sea.
Ocean thermal energy conversion plants can operate if the temperature difference between the surface and depths up to $2\text{ km}$ is ___ or more.
The warm surface-water is used to boil a ___ liquid, such as ammonia.
Geothermal energy is a type of renewable energy taken from the Earth's ___.
Extremely hot liquid and semi-liquid rock located under Earth's surface is called ___.
Biomass is renewable organic material that comes from plants and ___.
Biomass contains stored ___ energy.
___ combustion is the most common method for converting biomass to useful energy.
Thermochemical conversion of biomass includes ___ and gasification.
Biomass gasification produces a carbon monoxide and hydrogen rich gas called ___ gas.
The chemical conversion process known as ___ is used for converting vegetable oils into biodiesel.
Biological conversion includes fermentation to convert biomass into ___.
Anaerobic digestion produces natural gas, which is also called ___ or biomethane.
